steps.title: Translate the budget into an inequality
steps.content: Let $p$ be the pre-tax price per chair. The owner buys 81 chairs, so the pre-tax total is $81p$. A 7% sales tax multiplies this by $1.07$, giving an after-tax total of $81p \cdot 1.07$. This cannot exceed the 14,000-dollar budget: $$81p \cdot 1.07 \leq 14{,}000$$

steps.title: Solve for the maximum p
steps.content: Divide both sides by $81 \cdot 1.07 = 86.67$: $$p \leq \frac{14{,}000}{86.67} \approx 161.54$$ The largest listed pre-tax price that stays within the budget is 161.53 dollars.

steps.title: Compare with the choices
steps.content:
• 148.15 dollars is below the maximum, but it is not the greatest possible listed price.
  • 161.53 dollars stays within the budget because $81(161.53)(1.07)\approx 13{,}999.95$.
  • 172.84 dollars ignores the 7% tax.
  • 184.94 dollars applies the tax in the wrong direction.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
